LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE FEB-03-2025 HEARING RE: PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE; THE COURT FINDS THE COMMISSION DID NOT ABUSE ITS DISCRETION, AND THE APPEAL PRESENTED NO SUBSTANTIAL ISSUE AS TO THE PROJECTS' CONFORMITY WITH THE LCP (LOCAL COASTAL PROGRAM) (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). RESPONDING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER AND PROPOSED JUDGMENT PROVIDE TO ALL PARTIES FOR COMMENTS/OBJECTIONS AND PROVIDE TO THE COURT IN WORD FORMAT, AS THE COURT WILL ISSUE A FULL ORDER ON THIS DECISION. calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE (PER STIP OF PARTIES AND APPROVAL OF THE COURT)
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. MARK WOLFE, Esq. of M. R. Wolfe & Associates, P.C. (tel: 415-369-9400) for Petitioner Fort Bragg Local Business Matters.
Atty. TARA MUELLER, Esq. of the Office of the Attorney General of California (510-879-0754) for Respondent California Coastal Commission and specially appearing for the City of Fort Bragg.
Atty. JAMES MOOSE, Esq. of Remy Moose Manley, LLP (tel: 916-443-2745) for Real Party in Interest Best Development Group, LLC.

Hearing Re: PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court rules as follows:

The Court finds the Commission did not abuse its discretion, and the appeal presented no substantial issue as to the projects' conformity with the LCP (local coastal program). What the commission had to do was to take an appeal unless there was no substantial issue that existed with respect to the grounds on which the appeal was filed. The Commission reviewed the administrative record and issued a staff report that was ultimately adopted that found that there was a high degree of factual and legal support for the local government's decision. The Court's inquiry is limited to whether there is an abusive discretion in making that finding. Is that finding supported by substantial evidence? The Court finds it was supported by substantial evidence. As to the water quality management plan, the Court is satisfied that the administrative record does contain substantial evidence to support the commission's finding.

Responding party is to prepare a proposed order and proposed judgment provide to all parties for comments/objections and provide to the Court in word format, as the Court will issue a full order on this decision.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR# 13714 (tel: 415-601-7054 / email: apourtabib@gmail.com). (301/CVA)
HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE (CEQA MATTER)

HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)
